opp.name: Anora_S2P_2025
opp.next_step: Update from Anora - Deal moved to Q3 - Same Dear Valued Service Provider, Unfortunately, the original timeline will not hold and doesn’t make sense to revise as we are having too much uncertainty regarding this program. We have been building a robust business case, and we will now enter to internal discussions in terms of cost/benefits. We will be taking this case to our senior management during August and September 2026. This means that the program is “on hold” until September. Depending on the decision from our senior management we will give you update by end of Q3/2026. Attached still updated high level project plan showing timelines. Any questions please do not hesitate to contact us.
opp.ais_why:
• Feb 2026 Avoma call captures Anora Group's Ari and Lotta running a structured RFI process targeting a Mar 2026 release with POC, demos and reference checks built in. Casper from Zycus pitched AI capabilities including 'ask AI to create the RFI' as a positioning gambit, but the customer's focus during the conversation was on partner model, IT resourcing constraints, and implementation complexity ‚Äî operational concerns.
• Strong AI product list (Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake plus the full S2P suite including iCompliance and iSaaS) at $400K Formal Evaluation. BD_Engagement is Engaged. Senior management buy-in and funding have been secured per the call notes; a clear procurement-transformation path is in place. Multiple Anora stakeholders are engaged across the RFI process.
• Medium confidence: a structured RFI is happening but AI is not yet the explicit selection driver from the buyer's framing. Would move UP if the issued RFI mandates AI capabilities specifically. Defaulted DOWN at 6/7 boundary per rubric ‚Äî AI is bundled and acknowledged but not driving.

contacts.name: Ari Alm
contacts.title: Senior Procurement Manager - ICT & Digital Marketing

items.quote: the VP Sourcing and finance director prepare the EMT case but have never joined a Zycus call
business case is positive but no ROI, savings target, or cycle-time number has been shared
long list of different partners, your competitors included

metrics.narrative: Anora articulated clear operational pain (fragmented post-M&A procurement across Denmark, Sweden and Norway, manual tendering, labor-intensive sourcing, poor master data) that drives the S2P program (discovery call, 29 Aug 2025). The business case is complete and 'positive' with cost/benefit modeling under the finance director's review for the EMT (Ari Alm, 15 May 2026), but no specific quantified ROI, savings target or cycle-time reduction has been stated on any call or field. Ari flagged the case will be scrutinized because Q1 results were weak ('there will be a lot of hard questions', 15 May 2026), so co-authoring a quantified value narrative with peer CPG benchmarks (Heineken ROI) before the EMT session is the highest-leverage move.

sources: Ari Alm on all four calls (29 Aug 2025, 17 Feb, 15 May, 22 May 2026)
champion.narrative: Ari Alm (Senior Procurement Manager - ICT & Digital Marketing, ari.alm@anora.com) is a strong, active operational champion engaged consistently across all four calls (Aug 2025 to May 2026). He drove the RFI process, coordinated the business case, introduced Lotta into the evaluation, and is coaching Zycus on exactly what the EMT needs to see (a 5-10 minute benefits-focused video, Heineken example, no UI detail, 15 and 22 May 2026). He has access to the VP Sourcing and the finance director who own the EMT pitch. Strength is strong and active; the gap is that Ari is mid-level and we have no direct relationship above him, so building the upward connection to the VP Sourcing and finance director before the Aug-Sep decision is the champion-development move.

competition.narrative: No competitor has been named on any call, in any Salesforce field (Competitors__c is null), or in any Next_Step or Task. The 17 Feb 2026 call references a 'long list of different partners, your competitors included' receiving the RFI, but Anora deliberately will not disclose the shortlist to keep the process unbiased. All engagement has been cooperative with no pricing objection, feature gap, or reference to a rival's strength, and Ari requested Zycus demo materials for the EMT pitch, signaling Zycus is a serious and well-positioned contender. The named firms in calls (PwC, KPMG, EY, Deloitte) are implementation partners/auditors, not S2P competitors, and must not be treated as such. The wedge is our AI/Merlin differentiation and single-partner end-to-end delivery model (Anora's stated preference, 17 Feb 2026).

identify_pain.narrative: Anora's pain is operational fragmentation and manual-process inefficiency following recent M&A. On the 29 Aug 2025 discovery call they described fragmented procurement across Denmark, Sweden and Norway, manual and labor-intensive tendering, weak master data, and digital tools limited to reporting with no end-to-end automation ('I want to have less touch by people, rather more by machine'). The pain owner is the sourcing function, operationally driven by Ari Alm and Lotta (process harmonization). The Norway ERP migration completed Feb 2026 was a prerequisite, signaling this is a strategic post-integration capability build rather than a discretionary purchase, though buyer confidence is tempered by weak Q1 trading.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet visible: no MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire or procurement channel has been discussed on any call and no contract field is populated. Anora completed its ERP consolidation (Norway factory migrated onto unified SAP ECC, confirmed 17 Feb 2026), removing a major integration prerequisite, and prefers a single implementation partner to own delivery end-to-end (17 Feb 2026). The paper process will only activate after EMT approval in Aug-Sep 2026; until then it is genuinely unknown. Visibility would come from confirming the post-approval commercial/legal timeline and the intended procurement and InfoSec route with Ari.

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority is understood at the role level: senior management secured funding for the program (Lotta, 17 Feb 2026: 'we have now the buy in and the support from the senior management, so it is good to go, and we have the funding secured'), and the final cost/benefit approval sits with the relevant stakeholder Team, with the VP of Sourcing presenting alongside the finance director (Ari Alm, 15 May 2026). However neither the VP Sourcing, the finance director, nor any EMT member has ever joined a Zycus call, so we have no direct relationship with the economic decision body. Securing an introduction to the VP Sourcing and finance director through Ari before the Aug-Sep decision is the key access move.

decision_process.narrative: Anora is running a disciplined internal process the buyer laid out on 17 Feb 2026: eRFI to a longlist, evaluation and scoring, business case build, shortlist, a commercial/RFP and contract-negotiation phase, reference calls, then sign-off (targeted originally for Q4 2026). RFI scoring is complete but Anora is withholding feedback until senior management rules on the business case (Ari, 15 May 2026). The program is now formally on hold until Sep 2026 for an EMT cost/benefit decision, with an update promised by end Q3 (Next_Step__c). The approval chain is clear (VP Sourcing + finance director prepare the case, EMT approves), but the process is paused at the funding gate.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation is a structured RFI Anora authored themselves, deliberately declining Zycus's generic question template to keep the process unbiased ('we have the same offer from your competitors, and to keep the process non biased, I think it is better that we come up with our own requirements', Ari, 17 Feb 2026). Stated criteria include full S2P suite coverage, hands-on demo/testing of the environment, single-partner end-to-end delivery, references, and a positive business case (17 Feb and 15 May 2026 calls). The RFI was released around week 10 (early Mar 2026) and scoring is finalized (15 May 2026). The AI/Merlin story is acknowledged but not yet a confirmed selection driver (AIS 6, Moderate).

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Ari Alm is a strong, active operational champion engaged across all four calls, driving the RFI, business case, and EMT materials, and coaching Zycus on what the executives need. The single gap is upward reach: he is mid-level and we have no relationship with the VP Sourcing, finance director, or EMT who make the decision. Building that upward connection before Aug-Sep is the development move.

AI is curious, not hungry, on this deal.

Merlin is not yet landing as a selection driver, so the agentic story is under-positioned relative to its potential leverage.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Anora Group Plc — Formal Evaluation · $400K · closes 31 Dec

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 31 is carried by champion strength +1.00 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 52.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 16 is anchored on the stage win (31), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 6 / AIS_Status Moderate. Calls show AI acknowledged and mildly interesting to the buyer but not the decision driver; they even called their current CLM's AI 'quite dumb', an opening for a strong Merlin proof point. AI is curious, not hungry, on this deal.

items.change: Anora placed the S2P program on hold until Sep 2026 pending an EMT cost/benefit decision, with an update promised by end Q3
Executive introduction video delivered to Ari for the EMT audience; Ari confirmed he viewed it
Close date pushed from 31 Aug 2026 to 31 Dec 2026
RFI scoring finalized; Anora withholding feedback pending the business-case decision
Close date pushed from 11 May 2026 to 31 Aug 2026

deal_movement.summary: Momentum was healthy and multi-threaded through mid-May 2026, then Anora formally paused the program: the buyer is building a business case for a senior-management cost/benefit decision in Aug-Sep 2026 and the close date has slipped to 31 Dec. Last real buyer touch was Ari viewing the executive video on 3 Jun 2026.

items: Buyer requested and viewed the executive video (3 Jun 2026) - continued cooperation
Program formally on hold until Sep 2026 pending EMT decision - motion paused
Consistent four-call cadence and Zycus-specific material requests through May 2026
confidence_signals.summary: Buyer engagement was warm and collaborative through May but the program is now explicitly frozen until Sep and the last touch was 3 Jun. Not cooling in sentiment, but paused in motion.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 14 Aug 2025, ~10.7 months old, in Formal Evaluation throughout. Days to close 181 (close 31 Dec 2026, pushed from 31 Aug on 29 May). Last buyer touch 3 Jun 2026 (video viewed by Ari). Four-call cadence: 29 Aug 2025 face-to-face discovery, 17 Feb 2026 RFI roadmap, 15 May 2026 EMT-prep, 22 May 2026 video brief. Buyer explicitly placed the program on hold to Sep 2026 for a senior-management cost/benefit review; if approval lands late Sep that leaves ~13 weeks to run vendor selection, commercials, legal, InfoSec and close by year-end, which is only credible with immediate approval and no shortlist delay.
evidence: Anora paused the program for a senior-management cost/benefit decision in Aug-Sep 2026 and will update by end Q3 2026 (Next_Step__c, buyer email early Jun 2026)
RFI scoring finalized and business case built, awaiting EMT approval (Ari Alm, 15 May 2026 call)
Close date slipped 31 Aug 2026 to 31 Dec 2026 (field history, 29 May 2026)
No competitor named and no stakeholder conflict, but the economic decision body (VP Sourcing / EMT) has never appeared on a call
north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, cooperative evaluation that Anora has formally paused: the program is on hold until Sep 2026 while senior management runs a cost/benefit decision, and the close date has slipped to 31 Dec 2026 with the EMT gate not until Aug-Sep.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (appropriately cautious given the explicit hold and the Aug-Sep EMT decision gate)

flags: Multi-thread up before Aug-Sep: we only know Ari; get him to introduce the VP Sourcing and finance director who own the EMT decision.
Arm the business case: Q1 results are weak and the case will be scrutinized, so supply quantified peer-CPG savings and ROI benchmarks.
Protect the position during the pause: reinforce AI/Merlin and single-partner delivery so no undisclosed rival gains ground while the program is frozen.
best_practice_check.summary: Zycus is well-liked and single-threaded on a paused deal; the wins-strategy levers are getting above Ari to the EMT decision-makers, arming the business case with hard numbers, and reinforcing differentiation so a silent rival cannot slip in during the hold.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor has been disclosed. Anora ran a self-authored RFI to a longlist that includes rivals (17 Feb 2026) but has not named any on a call, in Salesforce, or in follow-ups, and has actively declined to share the shortlist to keep the process unbiased. Zycus is clearly in the qualified set (RFI scored, EMT materials requested) and the cooperative tone, absence of objections, and single-partner-delivery alignment suggest we are a strong contender or sole-preferred. The only real gate is the EMT's Aug-Sep budget decision, not a competitor. Treat this as a structured, criteria-led evaluation with silent rivals rather than inventing named ones.

items.deliverable: Provide the EMT cost/benefit decision (approve/reject the business case) and update Zycus

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is 6 (Moderate): AI is bundled across the full suite (Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake) and Casper pitched 'ask AI to create the RFI', but the buyer's framing is operational (partner model, IT resourcing, implementation) and they were skeptical of their current tool's AI. Merlin is not yet landing as a selection driver, so the agentic story is under-positioned relative to its potential leverage.

items.quote: our preference would be to get all the modules we have put in the RFI, and that's what we are proposing to the EMT
items.criterion: Full S2P suite (all offered modules) approved as one program

items.quote: We would prefer that one partner would take the full responsibility and run it end to end
items.criterion: Single partner takes full end-to-end delivery responsibility

items.quote: we would need kind of a 5 to 10 minute video, which would give to the senior management the understanding of the benefits of your tools
items.criterion: Executive-friendly, benefits-focused proof for the EMT decision

items.quote: The AI part is still a question mark ... it seems to me quite dumb, to be honest
items.criterion: Credible, differentiated AI value (buyer skeptical of current tool's AI)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Anora's stated expectations align well with Zycus: they want the full S2P suite approved as one program, single-partner end-to-end delivery, and an executive-friendly benefits story for the EMT. The open exposure is proving quantified value and AI credibility, since the buyer was underwhelmed by their current tool's AI and the business case faces scrutiny after weak Q1 trading.

• Feb 2026 Avoma call captures Anora Group's Ari and Lotta running a structured RFI process targeting a Mar 2026 release with POC, demos and reference checks built in. Casper from Zycus pitched AI capabilities including 'ask AI to create the RFI' as a positioning gambit, but the customer's focus during the conversation was on partner model, IT resourcing constraints, and implementation complexity ‚Äî operational concerns.
• Strong AI product list (Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake plus the full S2P suite including iCompliance and iSaaS) at $400K Formal Evaluation. BD_Engagement is Engaged. Senior management buy-in and funding have been secured per the call notes; a clear procurement-transformation path is in place. Multiple Anora stakeholders are engaged across the RFI process.

hard.next_step:
• Update from Anora - Deal moved to Q3 - Same

Dear Valued Service Provider,

 

Unfortunately, the original timeline will not hold and doesn’t make sense to revise as we are having too much uncertainty regarding this program. 

 

We have been building a robust business case, and we will now enter to internal discussions in terms of cost/benefits. We will be taking this case to our senior management during August and September 2026. 

 

This means that the program is “on hold” until September. 

 

Depending on the decision from our senior management we will give you update by end of Q3/2026. 

 

Attached still updated high level project plan showing timelines. 

 

Any questions please do not hesitate to contact us.

business_case.evidence: Business case built and described as 'positive' with cost/benefit modeling, but no quantified ROI, savings, or cycle-time numbers shared (Ari Alm, 15 May 2026)

gaps: Economic decision body (VP Sourcing / finance director / EMT) never appeared on a call; no direct relationship
No quantified ROI or savings figure shared by the buyer
Competitor shortlist deliberately undisclosed by Anora
